checklist.sql_injection: PASS — all DAO queries use Room @Query with bound :param placeholders; no string concatenation, no @RawQuery, no SupportSQLiteDatabase.execSQL with user input in production code.
checklist.hardcoded_secrets: PASS — no API keys, tokens, passwords, or connection strings in any reviewed file. Test fixtures use synthetic model IDs and timestamps.
checklist.fk_enforcement_runtime: PASS with note — ForeignKey(onDelete = CASCADE) declared on MessageEntity; SanctumDatabase.create() attaches a Callback.onOpen that runs 'PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON'. See finding sec-03 (defense-in-depth note: Room enables FKs by default, so this is redundant but harmless).
checklist.cascade_correctness: PASS — deleting a chat cascades to its messages; verified by MessageDaoTest.onDeleteCascadeRemovesMessages and by schema JSON (onDelete: CASCADE).
checklist.schema_export_safety: PASS — room.schemaLocation set to $projectDir/schemas (app/schemas), inside the module dir, not the build/ dir (so it is tracked in VCS as intended). Exported 1.json contains only DDL and column metadata — no secrets, no PII, no credentials.
checklist.dependency_supply_chain: PASS — Room 2.7.1 (stable GA, latest in 2.7.x line, no known public CVEs as of 2026-04-20). androidx.test:runner 1.6.2 and androidx.test.ext:junit 1.2.1 are current stable. All pulled from Google Maven.
checklist.privacy_columns: PASS for this scope — thinking_text/image_path/audio_path are nullable TEXT columns with no defaults, no PII leakage at the schema layer. Path construction and I/O are out of scope (Task 4).
checklist.database_file_location: PASS — Room.databaseBuilder with a plain filename 'sanctum.db' resolves to /data/data/app.sanctum.machina/databases/sanctum.db (app-private, MODE_PRIVATE, not world-readable on API 31+). AndroidManifest.xml has android:allowBackup="false" and android:dataExtractionRules, so the DB is not exfiltrated via ADB backup or device transfer.

findings.title: Database at rest is not encrypted (SQLCipher / Jetpack SQLite encryption not enabled)
findings.description: SanctumDatabase.create() uses plain Room.databaseBuilder without a SupportSQLiteOpenHelper.Factory that provides at-rest encryption (e.g. SQLCipher via net.zetetic:android-database-sqlcipher, or Jetpack androidx.sqlite with a passphrase). Chat and message rows — which can contain private user prompts, LLM replies, and paths to captured photos/audio — are stored unencrypted in /data/data/app.sanctum.machina/databases/sanctum.db. On a non-rooted modern Android device this file is not reachable by other apps, but it IS readable by anyone with physical access to a rooted/unlocked device, a custom recovery, or a forensic image.
findings.impact: If the device is lost, stolen, rooted, or seized, all chat history (including potentially sensitive user queries to an LLM and references to attachments) is readable as plain SQLite. No decryption key required.
findings.rationale_for_low: This is an intentional product decision (user-spec does not mandate at-rest encryption) and out of immediate scope for Task 1 (Room foundation). allowBackup=false and dataExtractionRules already blunt the most common exfiltration vector. Flagging as low so it is tracked as a future hardening item rather than ignored.
findings.recommendation: Decision candidate for a later phase: either (a) document explicitly in decisions.md that at-rest encryption is out of scope and users with high-threat models should rely on full-disk encryption + lockscreen, or (b) integrate SQLCipher with a key backed by the Android Keystore (BiometricPrompt / StrongBox) before the app ships chat history to production. No action required for Task 1.
findings.fix: Not required for this task. Recommend opening a follow-up ticket referenced from decisions.md.

findings.owasp: A09:2021 Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
findings.title: No audit trail for destructive DAO operations
findings.description: deleteById on both DAOs silently removes rows (and cascades messages) with no logging, no soft-delete column, and no undo mechanism at the persistence layer. For a local-only app this is acceptable, but there is no audit trail if a bug or malicious component (e.g., a compromised WorkManager worker in a later phase) wipes chat history.
findings.impact: Data loss from programmatic deletion is unrecoverable and untraceable. Not a confidentiality/integrity issue per se, but worth noting for availability (A).
findings.recommendation: No action for Task 1. When the UI layer lands (Task 8+), consider (a) soft-delete via a deleted_at column, or (b) logging destructive DAO calls through the existing app logger at DEBUG/INFO level so incidents can be reconstructed.

findings.title: ForeignKeysOnOpenCallback is redundant; FK enforcement test does not exercise the production code path
findings.description: Room has enabled 'PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON' by default on every new connection since Room 2.0 (see androidx.room.RoomOpenHelper). The explicit ForeignKeysOnOpenCallback in SanctumDatabase.create() is therefore redundant — it provides no extra enforcement beyond Room's built-in behavior. Separately, the instrumented test foreignKeyEnforcementEnabled() uses Room.inMemoryDatabaseBuilder without attaching the callback, so it proves only that Room's default behavior enforces FKs — not that the custom callback on the production builder runs. If the callback were ever silently broken (e.g., a future refactor removes addCallback), this test would still pass.
findings.impact: None at runtime — defense-in-depth is still in place because Room enables FKs by itself. But the test suite gives a false sense of coverage over the explicit callback.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) remove the ForeignKeysOnOpenCallback and rely on Room's built-in FK enforcement (documenting the choice in decisions.md), or (b) keep the belt-and-suspenders callback and add an instrumented test that exercises the production create() path — e.g., build via SanctumDatabase.create(context) against a temp file, introspect 'PRAGMA foreign_keys' on openHelper.readableDatabase, and assert it returns 1.
findings.fix: Not blocking. Either approach is acceptable; prefer (a) for simplicity.

findings.title: allowMainThreadQueries() used in instrumented tests — acceptable, noted for completeness
findings.description: Test setups build the in-memory DB with .allowMainThreadQueries(). This is the canonical Room test pattern and is fine in androidTest, but verify no production code (e.g., a future ChatRepository) copies it. Not a vulnerability, recorded as info so reviewers of Task 2+ know to flag it if they see it leaking into main/.
findings.impact: None in tests. Would be a performance/ANR concern, not a security one, if it ever appeared in main/.
findings.recommendation: No action. Keep an eye on main/ in subsequent tasks.

positive_observations: All DAO @Query statements use bound :param placeholders — zero SQL injection surface.
room.schemaLocation is pinned to $projectDir/schemas (module-local, VCS-tracked) and the exported 1.json contains no secrets, credentials, or PII — only DDL metadata.
ON DELETE CASCADE is declared at the schema level AND verified by an instrumented test (MessageDaoTest.onDeleteCascadeRemovesMessages), so orphan messages are structurally impossible.

AndroidManifest.xml already sets android:allowBackup="false", android:dataExtractionRules="@xml/data_extraction_rules", and android:usesCleartextTraffic="false" — the DB file cannot be exfiltrated via ADB backup or device-to-device transfer.
No hardcoded secrets, tokens, or credentials anywhere in scope — tests use synthetic model IDs like "m" and literal timestamps.

Room 2.7.1 is a current stable GA release with no publicly disclosed CVEs as of 2026-04-20; androidx.test:runner 1.6.2 and androidx.test.ext:junit 1.2.1 are the current stable versions from Google Maven.
Suspend + Flow only — no blocking DAO methods, reducing the chance of main-thread DB I/O that could trigger StrictMode disk-read violations in a debuggable build.
out_of_scope_noted: Attachment path validation (image_path, audio_path, thinking_text rendering) — Task 4 (ChatRepository) / attachment I/O.
At-rest DB encryption decision — product-level decision, not Task 1.
Migration strategy for v2+ schemas and projects/project_files tables — Task 1 explicitly excludes these (Decision 1, Decision 6 staging).
